vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1580: Dashboard shows a blank page on server restart instead of waiting for reconnect

plan.title: Dashboard shows a blank page on server restart instead of waiting for reconnect
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-8

narratives.Problem: On a server restart (pan reload/restart/redeploy) the dashboard goes blank instead of staying mounted and waiting to reconnect. The blank-pre-mount-document symptom (fix-direction #2) was already fixed on main by 31ef04dda (theme-aware boot shell + bundle-independent boot watchdog in index.html). The deeper regression remains: commit 3345c7c11 made RootErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch auto-window.location.reload() on GENERIC render crashes, which tears down the live mounted app on a restart-race crash; and there is no liveness signal on the Effect-managed /ws/rpc stream, so a silent half-open socket (laptop sleep / NAT drop) produces a permanent freeze with no error and no reconnect.
narratives.Proposal: Make the dashboard reconnect IN-APP without destroying the mounted document. (1) Add an app-level heartbeat to the /ws/rpc domain-events stream (a new SystemHeartbeatEvent in @panctl/contracts; server Stream.merges a ~15s tick into subscribeDomainEvents). (2) Add a client staleness watchdog that forces a fresh reconnect when ~2 beats are missed, catching half-open sockets. (3) Cap the reconnect backoff with exponential + jitter instead of a fixed 250ms hammer. (4) Wire the transport reconnect lifecycle to the visible overlay (export showOverlay + add hideOverlay; show 'Reconnecting…', hide on reconnect, show an actionable 'Server unreachable — Retry' after N failures). (5) Reverse the root-cause regression by narrowing componentDidCatch so generic render crashes no longer full-reload — only true module/asset-load errors do. (6, low priority) Surface the snapshot-fallback cliff instead of giving up silently.

autoDecisions.summary: Treat the 2026-06-07 issue comment's 5-step implementation plan as the authoritative spec, and add the generic-crash-auto-reload narrowing (issue root cause / fix-direction #1) as a sixth bead.
autoDecisions.rationale: The comment is the user's own root-caused plan with exact file/line locations; fix-direction #1 names the 3345c7c11 regression as the core cause, so reversing it must be in scope, not just the additive heartbeat work.

autoDecisions.summary: Do NOT re-do the index.html boot shell — it already shipped on main via 31ef04dda.
autoDecisions.rationale: Re-implementing would be redundant churn; git history confirms the dark boot shell + bundle-aware boot watchdog already satisfy fix-direction #2.

autoDecisions.summary: Server heartbeat interval = ~15s; client watchdog staleness threshold = ~35s (>= 2 missed beats).
autoDecisions.rationale: Matches the issue comment's stated numbers and guarantees a single dropped beat never trips the watchdog (avoids false reconnects on a healthy idle socket).

autoDecisions.summary: Add the heartbeat as a new DomainEvent union variant rather than a side channel; rely on the reducer's existing default case to ignore it.
autoDecisions.rationale: applyEvent already returns state unchanged for unrecognized types (`void (event as never); return state`), so no reducer change is needed and the frame still decodes on the typed wire.

autoDecisions.summary: Scope the snapshot-fallback-cliff bead minimally (surface the Retry overlay on window expiry) and order it last.
autoDecisions.rationale: The issue comment marks it optional/lower priority; the highest-leverage trio is heartbeat + watchdog + overlay.

items.title: Add SystemHeartbeatEvent to the @panctl/contracts DomainEvent union

narrative.Action: In packages/contracts/src/events.ts define a new SystemHeartbeatEvent Schema.Struct (type literal 'system.heartbeat', a timestamp string, and a small payload such as { ts: number }) and add it to the DomainEvent union (events.ts:1049). Rebuild contracts. Confirm the client reducer needs no change — applyEvent's default case (event-reducers.ts:1400) already ignores unknown types. The exact type literal and field shape chosen here are consumed verbatim by the server tick and the client watchdog, so this is the protocol foundation for those beads.

subItems.title: SystemHeartbeatEvent schema exists in events.ts with type literal 'system.heartbeat' and a timestamp + payload, and is exported (schema + type).
SystemHeartbeatEvent is added to the DomainEvent Schema.Union; `cd packages/contracts && npm run build` succeeds with no type errors.
Unit test (packages/contracts/src/events.test.ts) decodes a heartbeat frame through DomainEvent and asserts applyEvent leaves ReadModelState unchanged and does not throw.

items.title: Emit a periodic heartbeat on the subscribeDomainEvents stream

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/server/ws-rpc.ts, the WS_METHODS.subscribeDomainEvents handler (around ws-rpc.ts:484) currently returns eventStore.streamEvents.pipe(Stream.map(storedToDomainEvent)). Stream.merge a heartbeat stream into it: Stream.repeatEffect(emit a SystemHeartbeatEvent).schedule(Schedule.spaced('15 seconds')). The heartbeat is synthetic and must NOT be persisted to the event store. Touch ONLY the subscribeDomainEvents handler — leave subscribeIssueEvents and subscribeAgentOutput alone.

subItems.title: subscribeDomainEvents emits a SystemHeartbeatEvent approximately every 15s in addition to real events; real events continue to flow unchanged.
No heartbeat is written to the event store / no sequence is consumed for it; subscribeIssueEvents and subscribeAgentOutput handlers are unmodified.
typecheck and build of the dashboard server pass (npm run build).

items.title: Client staleness watchdog for half-open socket detection

narrative.Action: Reset a staleness timer on EVERY inbound frame (heartbeat or real event) in the wsTransport.subscribe() listener path / EventRouter. If no frame arrives within ~35s (>= 2 missed beats), force resetTransport() and reconnect. Critically, intercept event.type === 'system.heartbeat' at the very top of EventRouter.handleEvent: reset the watchdog and return immediately so the heartbeat never enters the sequence-based recovery coordinator (classifyDomainEvent) or pendingBatch.

subItems.title: Any inbound frame (heartbeat or real event) resets a ~35s staleness timer.
When no frame arrives for the staleness window, the client forces resetTransport() and a fresh reconnect (recovers a silent half-open socket that produces no stream error).
A 'system.heartbeat' frame resets the watchdog but is NOT passed to classifyDomainEvent/applyEvents and is not pushed to pendingBatch (verified by unit test).
Watchdog tests use vi.useFakeTimers() (no real-timer delays); one missed beat does not trip it, 2+ missed beats does.

items.title: Cap reconnect backoff with exponential delay + jitter

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/lib/wsTransport.ts, replace the fixed reconnect cadence (DEFAULT_RETRY_DELAY = Duration.millis(250) at ~line 113 used by Schedule.fixed(retryDelay) at ~line 215) with a capped exponential + jitter schedule, mirroring linear-client.ts:134. e.g. Schedule.exponential('500 millis').pipe(Schedule.either(Schedule.spaced('10 seconds')), Schedule.jittered). Account for the second reconnect path too: the onExit handler's setTimeout(run, 1000) (wsTransport.ts ~line 223) — align it so the two paths do not fight.

subItems.title: The Effect.retry schedule uses capped exponential backoff with jitter (cap ~10s), not Schedule.fixed(250ms).
The onExit/setTimeout reconnect path is reconciled with the new backoff (documented or aligned) so the client does not hammer the server during a long outage.
Backoff behavior is covered by a fake-timer unit test asserting increasing delays capped at the ceiling.

items.title: Wire socket-death to the visible reconnect overlay

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/recovery.tsx, export showOverlay (currently private, recovery.tsx:49) and add a hideOverlay that removes the overlay element. From the transport reconnect lifecycle (wsTransport.ts retry/onExit + EventRouter onReconnect), show 'Reconnecting to the dashboard…' once retries begin and hide it on the next successful reconnect (onReconnect). After N consecutive failed attempts, render an actionable 'Server unreachable — Retry' state (button calling resetTransport()/reload) instead of spinning silently. The app stays mounted throughout — no window.location.reload().

subItems.title: showOverlay is exported and a hideOverlay is added; hideOverlay removes the overlay DOM node and is idempotent.
On the first reconnect retry the 'Reconnecting to the dashboard…' overlay appears; on the next onReconnect it is hidden — without unmounting the app or reloading the page.
After N consecutive failed attempts the overlay shows an actionable 'Server unreachable — Retry' control instead of an indefinite spinner.

items.title: Narrow componentDidCatch: stop full-reloading on generic render crashes (root cause)

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/recovery.tsx, narrow RootErrorBoundary.componentDidCatch (recovery.tsx:161) so a generic (non-module-load) render crash NO LONGER calls waitForServerThenReload()/window.location.reload(). Keep the isModuleLoadError(error) reload path intact (legitimate new-hashed-chunk case). Generic crashes keep the document mounted and fall through to the in-app manual fallback render() (and/or the reconnect overlay). This reverses the 3345c7c11 regression. Remove the now-unused CRASH_RELOAD_KEY/cooldown machinery if narrowing makes it dead (clean up only your own orphans).

subItems.title: A generic (non-module-load) render crash no longer triggers window.location.reload(); the app shows the in-app fallback while staying mounted.
A module/asset-load error still self-recovers via waitForServerThenReload() (isModuleLoadError path unchanged); recovery.test.ts updated to assert both behaviors.
Any CRASH_RELOAD_KEY/cooldown code rendered dead by the narrowing is removed; no unused symbols remain; typecheck/lint pass.

items.title: Surface the snapshot-fallback cliff instead of giving up silently (low priority)

narrative.Action: In src/dashboard/frontend/src/components/EventRouter.tsx, the fallback poller stops silently after SNAPSHOT_FALLBACK_WINDOW_MS (3 min) without bootstrap success. When that window expires and bootstrap has still not completed, surface the actionable 'Server unreachable — Retry' overlay (reuse showOverlay from the overlay-wiring bead) rather than ending in a silent, frozen state. Keep the change minimal — this is explicitly lower priority.

subItems.title: When the snapshot-fallback window expires without a successful bootstrap, the UI surfaces the 'Server unreachable — Retry' overlay instead of stopping silently.
If bootstrap succeeds before the window expires, behavior is unchanged (no overlay shown).

items.title: Verify in-browser: server restart keeps the app mounted and self-recovers

narrative.Action: Browser UAT (isolated Playwright instance/profile — do not depend on another agent's session). Open the dashboard, restart the dashboard server, and observe: the page does NOT go blank, the app stays mounted, the 'Reconnecting…' overlay appears during the outage, and the dashboard recovers (overlay hides, live data resumes) once the server returns — all without a full white/blank document gap. Also verify the half-open case if feasible (kill the socket without server-side error) triggers the watchdog reconnect.

subItems.title: During a `pan reload`-style server restart the dashboard never shows a blank/white document; it stays mounted and shows the reconnect overlay.
After the server returns, the overlay hides and live data resumes without a manual refresh.
UAT runs in an isolated browser profile with no dependency on shared/other-agent Playwright state.
